content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reimagined as nested hierarchical communities of primitive micro-agents, where curiosity-driven exploration for useful new memory entries emerges spontaneously from inter-micro-agent competition instead of being hard-coded by a global controller. This framework reframes memory acquisition not as a pre-defined active sampling task, but as a self-organizing process where micro-agents compete to surface high-value unlabeled experiences that improve the overall agent's downstream performance.
